commonIssues:
A major issue is private land wrongly listed under Section 22A of the AP Registration Act, which bans the registration of government, assigned, or temple lands.

How to fix:

  • Check if the land is in the prohibitory list on IGRS AP. If wrongly listed, file a petition with the District Collector for de-notification. IGRS AP

Dotted lands—parcels with unclear ownership history often assigned during colonial times—lack clear documentation. This makes them difficult to register or sell.

How to fix:

  • Apply under the Dotted Lands Act via MeeSeva to remove the property from the dotted land register. MeeSeva

Ongoing re-surveys, specifically involving Land Parcel Maps (LPMs), have caused confusion. Mismatches between old records and new surveys frequently occur.

How to fix:

  • Request a fresh survey through the local Tahsildar via MeeSeva to resolve boundary issues.

An estimated 45% of mutation requests are initially rejected, often due to clerical errors, incomplete documentation, or "frivolous grounds".

How to fix:

  • Ensure all documents (Sale Deed, Pattadar Passbook) are properly notarized and matching.

Although many processes are digitized (e.g., via MeeSeva), landowners still rely on mediators due to the complexity of the 11-step registration procedure.

How to fix:

  • Follow the official IGRS AP step-by-step guides directly and avoid unauthorized agents.

Even after successful registration of a sale deed, the update in the ROR-1B (revenue record) does not always happen automatically, causing ownership issues later.

How to fix:

  • If auto-mutation fails, manually apply for Mutation (Webland update) at the Tahsildar office or MeeSeva. Webland Portal

The registered sale deed might not match with the Pattadar Passbook or Adangal (village account).

How to fix:

  • Apply for correction of land records in Webland via MeeSeva with the registered deed.

Old or missing entries in the Sub-Registrar records can lead to an incomplete EC, failing to show the clear title of the property.

How to fix:

  • Apply for a manual EC at the SRO if online records are incomplete.

Despite digital initiatives, the physical or final digital copy of the registered document can take time to be issued.

How to fix:

  • Track registration status online on IGRS AP.

In cases of inherited land, multiple people holding a single joint patta face challenges when trying to split it (sub-division) for individual sales.

How to fix:

  • Apply for land sub-division (FMB sub-division) via MeeSeva before selling.
